hyperedges.label: Access Domain Entity Model (users, roles, permissions and joins)

nodes.label: Two Parallel Authorization Systems
nodes.rationale: The solution deliberately runs two independent authorization stacks side by side: ABP's stock Identity/OpenIddict/PermissionManagement stack (governing Books/Authors and ABP admin pages) and a hand-rolled 'Access' system that is the actual subject of the project. They coexist on one principal because middleware merges the AccessCookie identity into ctx.User. Mixing checkers across the two systems returns wrong answers.

nodes.label: ABP Stock Auth Stack (Identity + OpenIddict + PermissionManagement)
nodes.rationale: Retained from the ABP startup template to govern the Books/Authors feature and ABP's own admin pages; permissions declared as ABP permission definitions and checked with [Authorize] / RequirePermissions.
nodes.norm_label: abp stock auth stack (identity + openiddict + permissionmanagement)

nodes.label: Hand-rolled Access System
nodes.rationale: Built from scratch with its own entities, password hashing, cookie scheme and permission checks so the project can demonstrate user/role management independent of ABP Identity. App* tables live alongside AbpUsers/AbpRoles but are unrelated.

nodes.label: AppPermissions (permission code constants)
nodes.rationale: Permission codes are plain strings (Users.View, Roles.Edit, ...) stored as rows in the AppPermissions table rather than ABP permission definitions, keeping the Access system data-driven and editable at runtime.

nodes.label: AppUserRole join entity
nodes.rationale: Composite-key join entity linking users to roles.

nodes.label: AppRolePermission join entity
nodes.rationale: Composite-key join entity linking roles to permission codes.

nodes.label: PasswordHasher (PBKDF2-SHA256)
nodes.rationale: Custom PBKDF2-SHA256 hasher with format 'iterations.salt.hash'. Deliberately not ASP.NET Identity's hasher, so hashes are NOT interchangeable between the two auth systems.

nodes.label: AccessDataSeedContributor
nodes.rationale: Seeds the permission rows plus the Administrator role and accessadmin user; run via DbMigrator rather than `dotnet ef database update` so seeding always accompanies migration.

nodes.label: Access Login Flow (/access-login → /access-signin)
nodes.rationale: Login.razor validates credentials via IAuthAppService then hard-navigates to the /access-signin minimal API endpoint, because a Blazor Server circuit cannot write an HTTP cookie. The endpoint signs into the AccessCookie scheme; /access-signout clears it.

nodes.label: AccessCookie authentication scheme
nodes.rationale: Separate cookie scheme for the Access system; middleware merges its identity into ctx.User so ABP's and Access's identities coexist on one ClaimsPrincipal.

nodes.label: UserRoleManagementBlazorModule.OnApplicationInitialization
nodes.rationale: Maps the /access-signin and /access-signout minimal API endpoints and registers the identity-merging middleware.

nodes.label: CurrentAccessUser
nodes.rationale: The correct injection point for identity/permission reads in Access UI code. ABP's ICurrentUser / IPermissionChecker must not be used for Access features because they resolve against the other authorization system and return wrong answers.

nodes.label: UserPermissionChecker.GetPermissionCodesAsync
nodes.rationale: Deliberately opens its own unit of work with requiresNew: true because Blazor circuits outlive the HTTP request whose DbContext would otherwise be reused (disposed-context risk). This must be preserved when editing.

nodes.label: Manual Page-Level Guarding Pattern
nodes.rationale: Access pages guard in OnInitializedAsync (check UsersView, redirect to /access-login if denied) and toggle buttons via _canCreate/_canEdit/_canDelete flags, instead of [Authorize] attributes, because [Authorize] resolves against ABP's permission system. Users.razor.cs is the reference implementation.

nodes.label: UserRoleManagementMenuContributor
nodes.rationale: Adds menu items conditionally using the same Access permission checker used by page guards, keeping navigation consistent with page-level authorization.

nodes.label: ABP Layered Architecture (Domain.Shared → Domain → Application.Contracts → Application → HttpApi/Blazor)
nodes.rationale: Standard ABP DDD layering. App services are auto-exposed as REST controllers via ConfigureAutoApiControllers, yet Blazor components inject the I*AppService interfaces directly in-process — avoiding an HTTP hop in the single-host deployment while keeping the remote HttpApi.Client option open.
nodes.norm_label: abp layered architecture (domain.shared → domain → application.contracts → application → httpapi/blazor)

nodes.label: Mapperly object mapping (Volo.Abp.Mapperly)
nodes.rationale: Mapperly (source-generated, compile-time) is used instead of AutoMapper (reflection-based) — see UserRoleManagementApplicationMappers.cs and UserRoleManagementBlazorMappers.cs.

nodes.label: UserRoleManagementDbContext with [ReplaceDbContext(typeof(IIdentityDbContext))]
nodes.rationale: Registered as a replacement for IIdentityDbContext so ABP Identity and OpenIddict tables share one database and one migration set with the Access and Books/Authors entities — a single migration pipeline instead of several.

nodes.label: Blazor Server + Blazorise + LeptonX Lite UI stack
nodes.rationale: Established page shape is a Blazorise `_modal` plus UiMessageService for CRUD. BlankLayout.razor exists to render chrome-free pages such as login.
nodes.norm_label: blazor server + blazorise + leptonx lite ui stack

nodes.label: DbMigrator Migration Workflow
nodes.rationale: Migrations are added from the EntityFrameworkCore project with Blazor as startup project. `dotnet ef database update` is forbidden because it skips the data seed contributors; DbMigrator applies migrations AND seeds.

nodes.label: UserRoleManagementEntityFrameworkCoreTestModule (in-memory SQLite)
nodes.rationale: Tests run against in-memory SQLite so no real SQL Server is required; the dev connection string in Blazor/appsettings.json points at a machine-specific local named instance.

nodes.label: Graphify Knowledge Graph Workflow
nodes.rationale: Prefer `graphify query/path/explain` over grep or reading GRAPH_REPORT.md because they return a scoped subgraph that is far smaller in context. Run `graphify update .` after code changes (AST-only, no API cost).

nodes.label: Layered DDD Startup Solution
nodes.rationale: Based on the ABP Application Startup Template with all fundamental ABP modules pre-installed.

nodes.label: UserRoleManagement.DbMigrator console application
nodes.rationale: Applies migrations and seeds initial data; must be run on first setup and after any new migration, in both development and production.

nodes.label: OpenIddict Signing Certificate Setup
nodes.rationale: Production requires an openiddict.pfx; two RSA certificates (one encryption, one signing) distinct from the HTTPS certificate are recommended.

nodes.label: abp install-libs (client-side dependency restore)
nodes.rationale: Automatic at solution creation, but must be run manually after cloning from source control or adding a new client-side package.

nodes.label: OpenIddict Encryption and Signing Credentials documentation
nodes.norm_label: openiddict encryption and signing credentials documentation

hyperedges.label: Access Domain Entity Model (users, roles, permissions and joins)
